title: I Tried Editing YouTube Shorts Entirely on Mobile for 7 Days
deck: Seven days, phone only — no desktop rescue. Trains, cafés, hotel Wi-Fi, overheating, exports at 99%, and the subtitle stack that didn’t murder my battery.
excerpt: We spent 7 days editing Shorts entirely on mobile in 2026 — testing subtitle workflows, exports, TikTok uploads, Safari issues, and whether phone-only editing is actually realistic.

metaDescription: We spent 7 days editing Shorts entirely on mobile in 2026 — testing subtitle workflows, exports, TikTok uploads, Safari issues, and whether phone-only editing is actually realistic.
ogTitle: I Tried Editing YouTube Shorts Entirely on Mobile for 7 Days
ogDescription: A honest 7-day diary: what worked, what broke on day 3, and whether mobile-only Shorts editing is realistic in 2026.

faqSchema.question: Can you edit YouTube Shorts entirely on mobile?
faqSchema.answer: Yes for simple talking-head Shorts, Reels, and TikTok clips — trim, caption, export vertical. Heavy multi-layer edits, long podcast clips, and 4K projects still fight phone RAM, heat, and export queues.

faqSchema.question: What is the best mobile workflow for Shorts?
faqSchema.answer: Record or import vertical, generate transcript/SRT in a lightweight browser tool, finish captions in CapCut or native app, export 1080x1920. Avoid full NLE timelines in mobile Safari when possible.

faqSchema.question: Do subtitle apps work well on phones?
faqSchema.answer: Native apps (CapCut, TikTok) work well for burn-in. Heavy browser subtitle editors often lag, refresh tabs, or stall exports. Text-first tools are less painful than giant timelines on phone.

faqSchema.question: Why do exports fail on mobile editing apps?
faqSchema.answer: Thermal throttling, background app kills, low storage, cellular timeouts, and server-side render queues. The 99% freeze is often a stalled job, not your phone lying.

faqSchema.question: Is Safari good for video editing?
faqSchema.answer: Safari is fine for lightweight text and SRT workflows. It is poor for hour-long uploads, full timeline editors, and long background exports — tabs refresh under memory pressure.

faqSchema.question: What tools work best for mobile creators?
faqSchema.answer: CapCut for finish, TikTok/Reels native for speed, Cutup or similar for link-to-SRT without a bloated editor. Split text and pixels instead of doing both in one heavy tab.

faqSchema.question: Can I generate subtitles on iPhone?
faqSchema.answer: Yes — use lightweight web tools for transcript and SRT, then import into CapCut. See our Safari and SRT guides for friction points.

faqSchema.question: Are browser subtitle tools better than apps?
faqSchema.answer: Browsers win for quick text and SRT; native apps win for touch-first burn-in and vertical export. Hybrid mobile stacks beat either alone.
